title: jasminum lanceolaria subsp. scortechinii
intro: Jasminum lanceolaria subsp. scortechinii is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae, commonly known as the fragrant jasmine, that is native to the tropical forests of Malaysia and Thailand. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Jasminum lanceolaria subsp. scortechinii include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate various health issues.
